excludes: WordPress whitespace, naming, documentation, and file-layout sniffs
justifiedExclusions.reason: The dependency-closed private lane is compiler runtime output behind native adapters; rewriting it would invalidate the compiler/runtime evidence and does not improve the WordPress-facing ABI. Correctness, security, compatibility, symbols, and autoload closure remain blocking.
Haxe 4.3.7 emits __hx__init as an isolated internal method; exact PHP 7.4 and 8.4 execution proves it, while the same compatibility sniff remains blocking on public PHP.

justifiedExclusions.scope: public-native PHP declarations and multiline closures
justifiedExclusions.reason: The generalized typed PHP printer currently owns these deterministic bytes and source ranges. PHPCBF must still prove that no non-excluded fixable WordPress formatting delta exists.

justifiedExclusions.scope: public-native class filenames, methods, parameters, and derived locals
justifiedExclusions.reason: Stable namespaced class files and Haxe-visible callback/export identities remain readable and reflection-safe; all WordPress correctness and security sniffs stay enabled.

justifiedExclusions.scope: includes/autoload.php fixed WPHX5201 and WPHX5202 messages
justifiedExclusions.reason: Production loader failures must remain observable; every message is compiler-owned constant text with no request, path, credential, or user data.
